principles: Markdown remains the source of truth.
Global installation requires uninstall or rollback verification.

safety_gates.description: Do not push repository changes without a direct human approval for that push.
Do not publish any package without a direct human approval for that publish action.
Do not distribute a public package before a repository license is selected.
Do not distribute a public package before stronger JSON Schema validation is in place.
Keep hook setup project-local unless a future global installer has rollback verification and explicit approval.
Any installer must have a verified uninstall or rollback path before user-visible write mode exists.
Any installer that updates existing targets must have verified backup and restore behavior before user-visible write mode exists.
Any downloadable release bundle must be verified from a clean extracted package before public distribution.
The public installer command remains preview-only; scratch-only verification may write and roll back inside temporary targets.

channels.label: Clone-local repository usage
channels.description: Use VibeLog from a local clone through npm scripts and direct Node entrypoints.

channels.label: GitHub release bundle
channels.description: Package a downloadable archive after clean extracted-bundle verification. GitHub release creation still requires explicit human approval.
forbidden_actions: creating a GitHub release without explicit approval

channels.label: Local installer scripts
channels.description: Dry-run-only public installer planner plus scratch-only rollback and backup/restore verifiers for temporary targets.

channels.label: npm package
channels.description: Future package-manager distribution after the data contract, license, package metadata, and publish dry-run are ready.

channels.label: Agent-specific templates
channels.description: Future helper packs for Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, AGENTS.md, and other agent environments.

next_recommended_slices.reason: Scratch backup/restore is now verified; future work can turn this into a user-visible installer design only after explicit approval.
Slice 20 reaches the planned GitHub push discussion milestone, but any push still requires a separate explicit approval.
A real installer write mode still needs a narrower approval UX, backup location design, and recovery messaging before it can exist.
